Usually, the first game each weekend is the 12:30pm kick off but there will be no early start this Saturday.

3 Man City are currently seven points behind Arsenal, with a game in hand

The first two games of matchweek 30 are scheduled for 3pm on Saturday, as the Premier League makes its return after a break for the FA Cup weekend.

Burnley will be hosting Bournemouth and Sunderland are set to take on Brighton.

But what is the reason for there being no early kick-off on Saturday? talkSPORT.com takes a look...

Why is there no 12:30pm game this weekend?

There was meant to be a 12:30pm start in the Premier League on Saturday, March 14.

However, there was a fixture change two weeks ago.

West Ham vs Manchester City was scheduled to take place in the early slot, with game live on TNT Sports.

But as Pep Guardiola's side played away in the Champions League against Real Madrid on Wednesday evening, the game was moved.

Man City released a statement on the enforced change, writing: "City travel to the London Stadium to face the Hammers on Saturday 14 March with the game now kicking off at 20:00 (UK).

"The match was moved to later in the day following the UEFA Champions League Round of 16 draw and the confirmation of our European schedule.

3 Arsenal will be hoping to extend their lead against Everton after equalising against Bayer Leverkusen midweek Credit: Getty

"We will face Real Madrid in the Spanish capital at 20:00 (UK) kick-off on Wednesday 11 March, with the second leg at the Etihad Stadium at 20:00 (UK) on Tuesday 17 March.

"TV viewers in the UK will be able to watch our match with West Ham on TNT Sports."

talkSPORT will also have live coverage from the London Stadium, in a massive game for both sides at opposite ends of the table.

Reshmin Chowdhury will be your host with commentary from Joe Shennan and former England striker Dean Ashton.

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.

Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

Premier League fixtures this weekend Saturday, March 14 Burnley vs Bournemouth, 3pm

Sunderland vs Brighton, 3pm

Arsenal vs Everton, 5:30pm

Chelsea vs Newcastle United, 5:30pm

West Ham vs Man City, 8pm - live on talkSPORT Sunday, March 15 Crystal Palace vs Leeds, 2pm

Man United vs Aston Villa, 2pm

Nottingham Forest vs Fulham, 2pm

Liverpool vs Tottenham, 4:30pm Monday, March 16 Brentford vs Wolves,8pm - live on talkSPORT

3 Wolves are back in action in hopes to pull off 'The Great Escape'

There may not be an early kick-off on Saturday, but there is still a full set of Premier League fixtures coming up.

There are surprisingly two games at 5:30pm, as Arsenal host Everton and Chelsea play Newcastle.

Then, on Sunday, there are four fixtures to look forward to, as Crystal Palace take on Leeds, Manchester United host Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest take on Fulham at 2pm.

And as Tottenham fight for their Premier League survival, their game is live at 4:30pm from Anfield as they battle Liverpool.

On Monday, Wolves travel to face Brentford, with that game also live on talkSPORT with Adrian Durham, Sam Matterface and former England midfielder Danny Murphy.

Football.title: Nottingham Forest v Fulham betting offer: Bet £10 and get £50 in free bets with BOYLE Sports
Football.content:
Nottingham Forest v Fulham betting offer: Bet £10 and get £50 in free bets with BOYLE Sports

Nottingham Forest host Fulham in the Premier League on Sunday as they try to take a big step towards survival.

The home side will be licking their wounds after suffering a 1-0 home defeat to Midtjylland in the Europa League, but will need to have minds fully focus on staving off relegation on Sunday.

And BOYLE Sports are now offering new customers £50 in free bets to those who place a £10 bet on any of the remainder of the football season, and 50% more winnings with a bet builder boost, an offer which can be claimed by following this link*.

Just a single point separates Tottenham in 16th (with 29 points) and West Ham in 18th (with 28 points), Forest sit in 17th also on 28 points from 29 games.



Forest and Tottenham clash in north London next weekend in what's being billed a huge six pointer in the relegation battle. But it might not mean too much to Vitor Pereira's side if they don't get a positive result against Fulham on Sunday.

Marco Silva's side are one of the most enigmatic teams in the Premier League. One week looking capable of mixing it with the top six, to the next slipping up to Championship opposition in the FA Cup.

News.title: Britain risks sliding into recession as economy records zero growth even before Iran conflict energy shock
News.content:
BRITAIN risks sliding into recession as the economy recorded ZERO growth even before the war in Iran kicked off, experts warn.

The UK could now go into reverse as high energy prices push up inflation hitting growth prospects and dealing a blow to Rachel Reeves.





The Chancellor insists there is “more to do” but the Middle East conflict is set to hit the UK further as the economy struggles for momentum.

Figures reveal the flatlining in January which was a fall from just 0.1 per cent in December. The three months to January showed growth of 0.2 per cent.

The manufacturing sector contracted by 0.1 per cent, construction only grew by 0.2 per cent and the services sector was flat in January.

A technical recession is recorded as two consecutive quarters of falling GDP.

But Tomasz Wieladek, from US asset manager T. Rowe Price, warned: “The UK has been one of the weakest advanced economies in terms of recent growth performance.

“Therefore, the current oil price shock will most likely not just lead to inflation, but also push the UK economy into recession, raising unemployment and reducing GDP.”

Martin Beck, chief economist at WPI Strategy, told The Sun: “The UK is not in recession yet, but it is far too close for comfort.

“Even though the energy price cap is set to fall by 7% in April, saving the typical household about £117 a year, that relief could prove short-lived if conflict in the Middle East keeps global energy prices high. With growth flat in January, the UK has very little room for error.”



Meanwhile, Ms Reeves said: “Our economic plan is the right one, but I know there is more to do.

“In an uncertain world, we are building a stronger and more secure economy by cutting the cost of living, cutting national debt and creating the conditions for growth to make all parts of the country better off.”

The Chancellor will use a speech next week to push for closer ties with Brussels in a push for greater growth.

Experts also say the Bank of England will hold off from cutting interest rates when policymakers meet on Thursday.

News.title: Fury as almost 40 per cent of new homes by 2030 set to go to migrants, analysis claims
News.content:
ALMOST four in ten new homes built by 2030 will be needed to house migrants, new analysis reveals.

Tories last night blasted the figure as proof Labour has maintained open borders “without any plan to deal with the consequences”.



Conservative Party research, based on the Office for Budget Responsibility’s latest Economic and Fiscal Outlook, shows the scale of pressure projected migration could place on Britain’s housing stock.



The economic watchdog expects net migration of 1,172,792 people between 2026 and 2030.

Using the ONS average household size, that would require about 499,000 extra homes purely to house new arrivals.

Over the same period Britain is forecast to add roughly 1.34 million new homes.

That means 37.1 per cent of all homes built in the next five years would go towards housing immigrants, rising to 39.1 per cent by 2030.

Government data also suggests the added demand could push up house prices, with migration pressures alone estimated to add about £9,489 to the average home.

Shadow Home Secretary Chris Philp said: “High immigration has real consequences for rents, house prices and who gets access to new homes.

“Labour has opened the door without any plan to deal with the consequences.

Shadow Housing Secretary Sir James Cleverly said: “Labour can promise a building spree, but the OBR’s own figures show more than a third of those homes will be absorbed by migration before local families even get a look in.



“That is why young people feel permanently locked out and local communities feel under strain.”

Mr Cleverly vowed to curb pressures driving housing demand by scrapping stamp duty and deporting all illegal migrants within a week.

A government spokesperson said: “We are pulling every lever to build 1.5 million homes, restore the dream of homeownership and end the housing crisis we inherited.

“This is alongside the biggest overhaul of Britain’s settlement model in 50 years, to tackle the unacceptably high levels of net migration in recent years which under this government, is down by nearly 70 percent.”

News.title: Labour minister denies cover-up over release of files on Peter Mandelson becoming US ambassador
News.content:
A MINISTER has denied suggestions of a cover-up over the release of files on Peter Mandelson becoming US ambassador.

The claims came as it emerged PM Sir Keir Starmer did not personally interview him for the post despite warnings over his links to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.





Energy minister Michael Shanks said the Government had complied fully with the demand of MPs and would be publishing further documents.

He insisted: “There’s been no cover-up at all. Downing Street is releasing the documents.”

Downing Street has said was “no requirement” for Sir Keir to formally interview Mandy.

But Tory leader Kemi Badenoch said: “The fact Keir Starmer did not even meet with Peter Mandelson before appointing him as Britain’s ambassador to Washington is a total dereliction of duty.

“Starmer likes to tell everyone how much of a hot-shot lawyer he was, yet he was handed a dossier clearly showing Mandelson’s close links to Epstein and didn’t even bother to interrogate the man he was about to appoint to high office.”

The Prime Minister’s official spokesman reiterated that Sir Keir and his team respond to advice in a “range of different ways”.

He said: “The absence of comments in a particular box doesn’t mean the advice wasn’t read or considered.

“There’s a range of different ways in which the Prime Minister and his senior team responds to advice throughout the day.”

News.title: Bosses will get incentives to hire youngsters under sweeping reforms to tackle unemployment crisis
News.content:
BOSSES will get incentives to hire youngsters under sweeping reforms to tackle the unemployment crisis.

Welfare Secretary Pat McFadden will next week announce a package to get nearly a million jobless young people into work.





As well as financial rewards for employers who take on youngsters, the reforms will include a shake-up of the apprenticeship levy.

A Government source last night said: “These are serious measures to tackle the Neets crisis, and help employers take young people on and train them for the future.”

Ministers are concerned that the levy, which is given to firms to hire youngsters, is being abused by bosses hiring graduates instead.

Mr McFadden is expected to defund some apprenticeship levy schemes that have been abused and fund others that are specifically aimed at 16 to 24-year-olds.

There are also hopes within Whitehall that the fall in net migration numbers will see youngsters fill the gaps left by exiting migrants.

Meanwhile, former Labour Health Secretary Alan Milburn is also investigating the issue and has said the UK faces a “lost generation”.

His report is due to be published this summer.

Figures show that nearly 28 per cent of so-called Neets are signed off work for sickness or disability.



But Mr Milburn has said that some of these conditions are normal and don’t prevent people working.

Worryingly, the unemployment rate for 16-24 year-olds in Britain is even above the Eurozone average.

Chancellor Rachel Reeves is also looking to slow down the increase to the youth minimum wage following a manifesto pledge to have a single rate for all workers over 18.

The hospitality and retail sectors have said the increase will reduce jobs for youngsters.

News.title: Ian Huntley bought Xbox game with chilling name which was delivered to prison 3 days after he died
News.content:
SOHAM killer Ian Huntley bought an Xbox game called Dark Souls: Prepare to Die — which was ­delivered to prison three days after his death.

Huntley had ordered the macabre fantasy video title before he was attacked with a metal pole in a jail workshop last month.





And it arrived on Tuesday this week, three days after Huntley died in hospital from injuries ­suffered in the beating.

A source said: “No one could believe it when it came into the prison on Tuesday.

“It was obviously the last thing he ordered and then it still arrived into the jail despite ­Huntley being killed.

“A lot of inmates heard it was on the delivery list and could not stop talking about it. Many were cracking sick jokes about it.



“They were commenting on the irony of it — and whether Huntley had a premonition about it or something.

“A couple of them even asked if they could have the game, but there are strict rules protecting a prisoner’s property when they die.”

In the game, players fight battles and collect souls and have to upgrade weapons to stay alive.

We revealed Huntley’s Xbox had been taken from him in January after contraband was found in his cell.



But he is understood to have won it back after behaving in the run-up to his death, aged 52.

Huntley, whose murder of pals Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham, Cambs, in 2002 horrified Britain, was bludgeoned in a brutal attack at HMP Frankland, County Durham, on Thursday, February 26.

He was left in an induced coma as he was treated at Newcastle’s Royal Victoria Infirmary.

But doctors, who consulted with Huntley’s mum Lynda Richards, switched off his life support and he died last Saturday.

Frankland inmate Anthony Russell, 43, is accused of murder.

News.title: Govt couldn’t run a bath, so why would they be any better running corrupt water companies…here’s what I would do, Keir
News.content:
TEN years ago, two old boys from Oxfordshire noticed that the normally crystal-clear river flowing through their sleepy little village had turned brown, and all the fish had died.

Most retired people in their shoes would tut and go off to do golf. But these guys, Ash Smith and Peter Hammond, decided to spend their days getting to the bottom of what was happening.



Their heroic and meticulous efforts have now been brought to life in a Channel Four three-parter called Dirty Business. And this isn’t Mr Bates versus the Post Office. It’s way bigger than that.

I cannot recommend it highly enough because I reckon that what Ash and Peter discovered is almost certainly the biggest crime in British history.

They found out that Britain’s water companies have not been putting raw, untreated sewage into the nation’s rivers when heavy rainfall had overwhelmed the system.



They were doing it constantly. Everywhere. So they were taking the money we gave them . . . . and what, using it to charter private jets for themselves?

The story left me stunned and also a little embarrassed because it turned out that Ash and Peter had written to me last year, to explain what they’d found and I hadn’t even bothered to read their letter.

Like everyone really, I just assumed the problem with Britain’s water really only affected a few surfers, the odd wild swimmer and Feargal Sharkey.

And as I’m none of those things, I figured it wasn’t very important.

How wrong I was, because it is massively important. The water companies are trousering billions. Literally billions. And Dirty Business tells us children are dying because they’re not spending any of their ill-gotten gains on infrastructure. Put simply, it’s cheaper to dump a turd into a river than it is to process the turd and render it harmless.



Of course, as a result of the Channel 4 show, there are now extremely loud demands for the whole water industry to be nationalised.

People have it in their heads that the Government would do a better job, but this is nonsense. You only have to look at the potholes and the NHS waiting times, and the immigration system to understand that.

The Government couldn’t even run a bath. I mean, would you drink tap water if you thought David Lammy had produced it?

The fact is that there’s nothing wrong with privatised business. My local shop is privately owned. So is my local garage. And so is Timpsons, the key cutters with more than 900 outlets around the country. And none of those guys are killing children for profit.



And anyway, as the show makes clear, Government officials are part of the problem.

They refused to look into the sewage problem and if they were ever cornered, they just blamed farmers. That bit in the show made me seethe, quite noisily.

So, no. The water companies most definitely should not be privatised.

But if I was Sir Starmer, I would insist that the current bosses, and their families, are all sent to jail for 400 years.

This would send out a clear message to the people who replace them that if one turd or one tampon is released into any stream, anywhere, ever, they too would be in the clink for the rest of their lives.



TAX 'EM BY THE TONNE WHEN I started to drive, most cars weighed less than a tonne. Whereas now, thanks to the new hybrid drive systems, the average family saloon weighs more than two tonnes. And you wonder why there are now so many potholes on our roads. In the past, cars have been taxed depending on how expensive they are or how much power they produce or even what sort of fuel they use. Well now, there needs to be a tax on flab.

Formula 1 isn’t so Super, Mario

A NEW era of Formula One motor racing got under way in Australia last weekend, and I couldn’t believe my eyes.

I’d worried that the new cars, with their hybrid drive systems and their boost buttons and their snazzy little batteries, would be too complicated.

Plus. That “boost” button which makes you go faster for a short period works well in Super Mario Kart when you want to get past Yoshi and you’ve run out of toadstools. But this is Formula One. And when someone makes an overtaking move, I want to believe it was his skill and his testicles that made it possible. Not some gizmo in the software.



To begin with, however, it looked like I’d worried unnecessarily. One car didn’t make it to the start line before crashing. Two didn’t get out of the pits.

And then, when the lights went out and the race began, some cars left the line like they were Austins from the 1970s and some like they were being propelled by a Star Trek special effect.

The action didn’t let up either. In the first ten laps, Charles Leclerc in the Ferrari and George Russell in the Mercedes swapped places for the lead a staggering seven times, and I genuinely thought I was witnessing the birth of something fantastic. The Red Bull expired in a cloud of smoke, and we haven’t seen that sort of thing for years. And then a Cadillac ground to a halt as well. It was all very exciting.

Formula One was back. And I was glad I’d set my alarm for crikey o’clock so that I could watch it all unfold, live. But then, half way through the race, it became obvious that the drivers had worked out how to use all the new buttons.

They’d realised that if they used their boost facility to make an overtaking move, they’d have no power left and that the car they’d just overtaken would soon be back in front.



Meanwhile, the new young drivers like Arvid Lindblad were dealing with the technology better than the old hands, because the steering wheel is now nothing more than a PlayStation controller.

And that’s something they’ve grown up with.

This means they know instinctively that if they press the yellow button, a banana skin is dropped on to the track in their wake.

Which is great for them. But do I want to get up in the middle of the night to watch a bunch of teenagers playing a video game?

I’m not sure I do.



TB, OR NOT TB

AFTER seven months of lockdown, Diddly Squat farm became officially TB-free this week.

But before we had a chance to celebrate, we found out that one of our donkeys has laminitis and must be put down.

In farming, it seems you are allowed one bit of good news, but it must always be accompanied by some kind of disaster.

JUST the other day, Sir Starmer said that one of our shiny new aircraft carriers would soon be heading to the Eastern Mediterra-nean.



But of course, he has now done a U-turn and says that it will not be going to the Eastern Mediterranean after all.

Apparently, it will be heading to the Arctic.

Nope. Me neither.

I WAS forced this week to watch Hamnet, the new film about . . . actually I have no idea what it’s about.

Shakespeare’s work is dull enough at the best of times but a film about the man is even worse.



I haven’t been as bored since James May once tried to explain torque.

News.title: Mystery as man’s body pulled from river after loud music and splashing heard as pals remember him as a ‘good person’
News.content:
WATER TRAGEDY Mystery as man’s body pulled from river after loud music and splashing heard as pals remember him as a ‘good person’

MYSTERY surrounds why a man got into a river after his dead body was pulled from the water, an inquest has heard.

Paul Yeomans died after entering the river on Riverside Road in Norwich on August 29 last year.





Police, fire, and ambulance crews rushed to the scene of the incident.

But despite their best efforts to resuscitate him, the 37-year-old was pronounced dead at the scene.

Paul’s death was listed as drowning alongside the ingestion of alcohol.

An inquest into his death took place on March 13 at Norfolk Coroner’s Court.

During the proceedings, a witness said that they heard loud music and splashing noises in the vicinity.

The noises were described as similar to someone struggling to swim.

A bicycle and bag of clothing, believed to be Paul’s, was found on the river bank.

The court also heard from Paul’s friends who described him as a “good person” and someone who wanted “so much more than his demons allowed”.



The inquest heard that he had a history of heavy drinking and drug use, such as heroin, but was able to restrain himself from the latter.

Ethanol levels of 144mg were found in his blood during a toxicology examination, which was believed to have a significant effect on his cognitive function.

Paul was found to have worked with a health and social care charity, Change Grow Live, for his addiction issues.

But no evidence suggested that he had any mental health admissions or had thoughts of self-harm.

If you are affected by any of the issues raised in this article, please call the Samaritans for free on 116123.



Assistant coroner Robin Weyell concluded the inquest by stating that there was insufficient evidence as to why Paul entered the water.

He added that there was also no evidence that he had suicidal thoughts.

Mr Weyell ended the inquest by giving his condolences to those that were affected by Paul’s passing.

News.title: I DID meet Huw Edwards & spend night in hotel – he tried to touch me & I froze, reveals teen he paid for sexual pics
News.content:
THE vulnerable teen paid for sexual images by Huw Edwards has revealed for the first time that they did meet in person.

In a bombshell chat with The Sun, the victim, now aged 22, says Edwards “bribed” him into going to a hotel room where they spent the night together.





Speaking ahead of a Channel 5 drama based on The Sun’s unmasking of Edwards, he also revealed:

How Edwards would pleasure himself during video chats while in his family home.

How his drug addiction spiralled as he struggled to cope with the pervert’s increasing demands for explicit photos and videos.

How arrogant Edwards said he would “sue everyone” — including his victim’s parents — when The Sun first exposed him.

Edwards, one of the Beeb’s highest-paid newsreaders, was exposed by this paper in July 2023, after we reported he paid the then 17-year-old £35,000 for ­sexual pictures.

In September 2024 he was given a six-month suspended sentence after admitting having indecent images of children.

The young man has now told how he met with Edwards at the Marriott hotel in Cardiff after the ex-presenter threatened to stop paying him money.

He recounted: “We did meet up once. At this point he was getting my hotels as I was not going back home and I was relying on him.

“He said if I didn’t come he wouldn’t help me any more. He was standing outside the hotel with his hands in his pockets waiting for me.

“We went through the lobby and he walked through like it was nothing. I could feel people looking.



“Maybe because it was him, or maybe it was because he was with me as I had just turned 19.

“It was like two years after we had been talking. I couldn’t speak as I was just so nervous.

Watch an extended version of Scarlet’s interview with Huw’s victim on YouTube

“When we got to the room he tried to put his hand on my shoulder and I froze. I couldn’t get my words out. I just laid on the bed and went to sleep so he couldn’t do anything.

“He left in the morning as he had work.”



Edwards’ victim, who we have never named, said Edwards tried to meet him several more times — but he managed to avoid it.

He said: “There was one time when my stepdad tried to confront him at the train station and I said I would meet him but I didn’t.

“I didn’t even know my stepdad was going to see him. This was when my mum saw the messages as they had come through to her phone which I was logged into. I didn’t meet him as I was taking drugs.

“And there was another time when I was meant to meet him and I didn’t.

“He gave me his room number as he was in Cardiff, and he waited up until 6am or 7am.



“I was hoping he’d just go to sleep but he didn’t.

“He messaged me every single hour to ask where I was and I would just make excuses. I must have had 50 messages from him that night.

“He was like, ‘Where are you? You’re taking the p***’.



“He was saying I was lying and I wasn’t coming.”

The young man tells how he was groomed by Edwards as soon as the BBC star first made contact, after we revealed yesterday that the two were introduced through the newsreader’s paedophile pal Alex Williams.

He said: “Within five minutes of me messaging Huw after Alex Williams introduced us, he told me to set up a PayPal account and gave me £500.”

Edwards soon started asking for explicit images of the vulnerable teen in exchange for cash.

He said: “I can’t remember when the pictures started but I remember the first time he got weird.



“I was really upset as I was having family problems and I rang him up crying. I said I didn’t have anywhere to stay and we were Facetiming. I was really sobbing.”

He added: “Huw got on to his chair and put his hands on his legs.

“When I was ending the call he said, ‘I thought you were going to do something for me’. That’s the first time I knew something wasn’t right and it made me feel weird. I was really vulnerable.

“Now I see it was an easy way to take advantage of me.”



The young man said Edwards began to request snaps — making him feel obligated rather than asking.

He explained: “It was always done in a roundabout way.

“He would make sure I knew that it was expected. He was very careful as he was very worried about people using his words against him.

“He would make me feel guilty and I would think, well, he did help me, he did give me money, he did book me a hotel. That’s how he gets you.”

It was Edwards’ money, the young man explained, that caused him to spiral into a drug addiction.



He said: “I sent him pictures loads of times. The last one was a month before The Sun broke the story.

“Huw knew I was spending it on drugs. He would insult me sometimes when we were arguing about me taking drugs.”

Edwards’ victim added: “Sometimes he would ask for specific things and send me pictures of him too.

“He even pleasured himself in his family home on video calls. I had to take drugs when I was taking the pictures as it was easier to cope.”

The young man revealed how Edwards sent thousands of messages — many of them while sat behind the famous BBC News desk.



He said: “I used to message him and say to my friends ‘look at this’ when he was on TV presenting the news. As soon as the camera panned off him he would view my message and message me back.

“He’d obsessively message. He was on WhatsApp 24 hours a day. He would be up at 4am online.

“He would always see my message straight away so he must have been addicted to messaging people.”

When our story broke in July 2023, Edwards rang the young man begging him to stop the story.

He said: “Before The Sun published the story — when he was approached by the BBC and suspended — he rang me and asked me who had told them about us.



“I asked my mum and she told me it was her but I was too scared to tell him so I just denied it until he found out. Then he lost it.

“He threatened me with suicide. Then all of a sudden I didn’t hear from him so I thought he had killed himself.

“He was playing the victim. He messaged me saying, ‘I don’t think I can do this any more, baby’ and how he wanted to die.



“I begged him to stop and ­comforted him. Then he said he would pay my stepdad and mum to stop the story. He didn’t give a ­figure but he asked me to ask them how much they wanted.

“I said, ‘Just take the money and it will all go away’ — but my mum didn’t even consider it. I didn’t realise the web I was stuck in. I just thought she would get money out of it. I had grief on both sides — but I was on the wrong side.”

The young man initially denied The Sun’s revelations via expensive lawyers.

But he explains: “He gave me a lawyer’s number and name and said to message him as he will know what to do.

“I did ask to say sorry to his wife through the lawyer, though, as I felt like it was all my fault at the time.



“If he hadn’t got arrested I wouldn’t have snapped out of it.”

He also reveals how Edwards reached out to him even after being suspended by the BBC and had raged about our original story.

The young man said: “He wasn’t meant to be speaking to me but he wanted to talk on the phone.

“He wanted to know if I had said anything. He wanted to sue my mum and stepdad. He said he wanted to sue everybody.”

Reflecting on his time with Edwards, the young man said: “He took advantage as I was naive. He definitely knew what he was doing with the money.



“It felt addictive. Every time he deposited cash I would think, oh, he still cares about me.

It was a rush with the money, like gambling. It made me feel special when he was giving me money. He called me princess and I thought, he does care for me.

“He’d get me so reliant on him and be funny if I didn’t do what he wanted.

“If I asked for something and he hadn’t got anything for a while, as in me sending him pictures, he would be weird.

“It was a control thing. And then when we hadn’t spoken for a while he would get really nervous that I was going to tell someone and so he would be extra nice.”



The young man added: “I’m getting on with my life. I don’t take drugs. I’m a different person.

“I am traumatised but I’m sort of glad it did happen because I came out the other side. I proved to myself I’m not like how everyone thought I was going to turn out.”

In December, Edwards posted a portrait on Facebook, in what some saw as a bid to rehabilitate himself, but he later deleted it after outrage from victims’ families and MPs.

The young man said: “He was testing the waters to see if people have got over it but he’s never going to be the face of any show again.

“When this drama comes out, it will shatter any ideas of a comeback. It will send him on a spiral.



“He struggles to hold it together privately as he’s an angry person.

“I don’t think he’s sad. I think he’s more resentful about what’s happened. He’s not taken accountability for what he’s done.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I’m hoping that if there are other people out there this will make them come forward.”

News.title: Inside Magaluf hotel where Brit was raped by 'wolf pack' as major update emerges
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Inside Magaluf hotel where Brit was raped by 'wolf pack' as major update emerges

WARNING: DISTRESSING CONTENT. As the Magaluf 'wolf pack' rapists are sentenced, the Mirror takes a look at the BH Mallorca hotel, where the horror assault occured just a short walk away from the lively strip

View 8 Images The Magaluf 'wolf pack' rapists have now faced sentencing

Located just a nine-minute walk from the Playa de Magaluf beach, BH Mallorca was once a popular hotel amongst young, lively holidaymakers looking to make the most of the sunny Spanish town's famed party scene.

‌



But in the summer of 2023, it became known for a very different reason, with a brutal group rape taking place in room 108, just as dawn was breaking over the nearby Magaluf strip.

‌



An 18-year-old girl was attacked by a "wolf pack" of men at the hotel, who laughed and hurled vile insults as they filmed the degrading assault. The twisted gang even reportedly invited complete strangers in from the corridor to join them, offering "free sex".

‌



The harrowing incident has shocked locals and tourists alike, and now it has emerged that other guests have expressed concerns about safety at the adults-only resort, complaining of break-ins, "dirty" conditions, and out-of-control visitors.

View 8 Images Once famed for its raucous pool parties, a more family-friendly venue now sits on the site

The Mirror can reveal that BH Mallorca, as it was once known, is no more, and the foam parties and all-night drinking have long stopped. The hotel closed its doors in 2024, just over a year after the horror incident, with the final "legendary BH pool party" marking its end. But the memories of the hotel continue to haunt those who once stayed there.

‌



Once part of the Palladium Hotel Group, it had since been transformed and rebranded to the Fergus Club Mallorca Waterpark by the Fergus Group, which didn't own the site in question in its previous iteration.

With pool parties, resident DJs, and a swim-up bar stocked with cocktails, it's easy to see why the hotel attracted so many fun-loving twentysomethings over the years, with its proximity to super-club BCM being a particular draw. Taking to Facebook in March 2024, DJ Tom Zanetti said of its closure: "I have such amazing memories, honestly, what a day it was every single time. I don't care what anyone says, it was the best pool party in the world."

But a number of those who've stayed at the resort were horrified by their experience - and the conditions.. Complaints ranged from hygiene grievances, with reports of "dirty prison-style blankets" and unflushed poo in the toilets, to the "utter dog dirt" food that one person claimed had been left out all day, as well as grave safety concerns.

‌



8 View 8 Images Views from certain balconies left plenty to be desired 8 View 8 Images The food has been described as 'utter dog dirt'

The array of one-star reviews read more like chapters in a particularly disturbing Stephen King novel. One person wrote: "The services at the hotel were actually decent, if you were on death row with the hope of ending it all as soon as possible."

‌



Another alleged: "We were constantly met with demoralising and appalling services that barely met basic human rights and were reminiscent of third world countries".

Amid the photographs of seemingly undercooked pork and shabby paintwork, there were a number of concerns raised over the standards of safety in place. One furious former guest warned: "A third issue we had with the room is someone broke into it and strangely did not take anything but left all their belongings.

"When we told reception and security, they were not phased and did not offer any help at all. Obviously, two of us did not feel safe in that room, knowing someone had been in and could get back in, so they had to stay in our room again. We went and told one of the reps the next morning, and he again offered no help; he just said, 'Drunk people do weird things'. None of the reps acknowledged us whilst we were there, apart from on the last day when we were waiting to leave."

‌



8 View 8 Images One guest described the security as 'mediocre' 8 View 8 Images Some guests claim their rooms were broken into

Another claimed: "Security was mediocre. There are signs up advising against illegal ticket sellers operating in the area - these sellers were allowed to enter the hotel and knock on our door trying to pitch us tickets to a bar crawl, which may or may not have actually existed."

‌



And a third person alleged: "When me and my friend returned from our night [out], we came to our room to find a boy's clothes in our room, and some of our belongings taken. Luckily, nothing in the safe but chargers etc were taken. We went to inform reception and the guard said he saw the door locked on his 'patrol' but not once the whole week did he ever patrol they just stand in reception. No one was concerned that someone had broken into our room, and we felt unsafe, so once again we had to stay with our friends."

View 8 Images Some guests reported feeling 'unsafe'

There were also various mentions of locks being broken in both bedrooms and bathrooms, while one individual claimed to have "witnessed a young lady looking scared and confused as three big security staff surrounded her, pushed her around and bullied her while she was in the reception area late at night."

‌



Although it stood in a renowned party area, the noise appears to have been unbearable for many guests, with reports of 4 am chanting and "screaming" until 5 am. One person wrote: "If you're expecting to get any sleep while you're here, think again. There are people screaming and shouting for the entire night, unless you're in a coma, sleep is unlikely."

Others who'd requested balconies were deeply disappointed by the uninspiring views of "a dirty smelly alley and dumpsters".

View 8 Images Hygiene grievances, included reports of 'dirty prison-style blankets'

‌



Nowadays, the party-heavy site has undergone a significant rebrand and is instead known as the Fergus Club Mallorca Waterpark Hotel. A world away from BH Mallorca's intense eight-year run, this new and updated venue focuses on family-friendly holidays, complete with candy coloured slides for little ones and even a kids' club.

Instead of stag parties and rowdy 21sts, families now fill the rooms of the hotel, which is regularly praised for its cleanliness. There are, however, still some complaints about the noise that spills over from the streets of the party town, which may take some time to revamp its image.

The Mirror has reached out to the Fergus Group and the Palladium Group for comment.

Article continues below

If you've been the victim of sexual assault, you can access help and resources via www.rapecrisis.org.uk or calling the national telephone helpline on 0808 802 9999

Do you have a story to share? Email me at julia.banim@reachplc.com

News.title: Beatrice and Eugenie 'furious for being left out in cold' over parents' Epstein links
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Beatrice and Eugenie 'furious for being left out in cold' over parents' Epstein links

The royal sisters are understood to be furious after being dragged into the scandal involving notorious sex offender Epstein, after it emerged they traveled with Sarah Ferguson to see him days after his release from prison

View 4 Images Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie are understood to be unhappy at being dragged into the Epstein scandal (Image: Justin Goff Photos/Getty Images)

Former prince Andrew's daughters are furious they have been “left out in the cold” following their parents’ involvement in the Jeffrey Epstein scandal.

Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie are understood to be angered over Buckingham Palace’s unwillingness to back them publicly, after details emerged linking them to the late convicted paedophile. Royal sources suggested a “distinct cooling of attitudes” towards the two princesses in recent weeks.

It emerged earlier this year from the so-called Epstein Files, part of the three million documents released by the US state department in January, that the sisters had accompanied their mother Sarah Ferguson to the United States to meet Epstein. Just days earlier, he had been released from prison in 2009 for soliciting a minor for prostitution. The princesses were 19 and 20 years old at the time.

There is no suggestion of wrongdoing by either Beatrice or Eugenie, while their father has always denied any wrongdoing.

King Charles hails groundbreaking sustainable innovations at global summit READ MORE :

View 4 Images Princess Beatrice, Sarah Ferguson and Princess Eugenie flew to the United States to meet Jeffrey Epstein after his release from prison (Image: GETTY)

Previously it was only known that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and his wife had a close personal relationship with Epstein. But emails from Fergie’s assistant suggest Beatrice and Eugenie were keen to join their mother on the trip, leading to Ferguson asking the billionaire financier to fund first class seats for her and business class for the princesses.

A royal source said: “Whatever way you look at it, it is a very uncomfortable reality that the entire York family indulge themselves at the expense of Epstein.”

While the King invited both Beatrice and Eugenie to his Sandringham estate at Christmas, to spend the festive period with the extended royal family, both Andrew and Sarah were united having been just weeks earlier stripped of their titles and honours owing to the growing Epstein scandal.

Their invitation and acceptance was seen as a bold statement of support from the King who had sympathy for their plight. However, royal sources suggest the situation has now changed drastically.

The source added: “The sisters are extremely unhappy. They feel they have been left out in the cold. They aren't being supported by Buckingham Palace and are certainly concerned they will lose their titles and privileges as a result of their parents’ conduct.”

View 4 Images Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie are understood to be unhappy at being dragged into the Epstein scandal (Image: Getty Images)

The sisters, who are not full-time working royals, still maintain homes on royal estates as part of historic deals arranged by their father. Beatrice has an apartment in St James’s Palace and Eugenie has Ivy Cottage, a three-bedroom property at Kensington Palace.

The deal, a private arrangement between the Yorks and the King, requires them to pay an undisclosed sum in rent. It is unclear whether the deal is similar to the arrangement the former Duke of York had for his 30 room Royal Lodge mansion, where he was required to pay “one peppercorn if requested” after an initial rental agreement.

While there is no suggestion at this stage that the King will strip them of their titles, those in royal circles suggest there is a “deep unease” about releasing any form of public statement regarding their princesses as allegations relating to the Epstein files continue to emerge.

Recent reports suggested the King would not invite them from Royal Ascot in June, where the royals typically gather en masse during the five day festival.

The Mirror understands that no decision on Beatrice or Eugenie's attendance has been made, however, if they were to attend they would not ride in the carriage procession.

View 4 Images The family in happier times, before the Epstein scandal unfolded (Image: Instagram)

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Sarah Ferguson are deeply mired in allegations of misconduct surrounding their relationship with the late Jeffrey Epstein following his conviction for sex offences.

Andrew was arrested on February 19, his 66th birthday, on allegations of misconduct in a public office, over claims he passed confidential information to Epstein during his role as a trade envoy. He is currently under investigation.

The King and Queen have previously stated they were “profoundly concerned”, and when Andrew was arrested the monarch said “the law must take its course”.

A total of 10 police forces are assessing a range of claims that the former Duke of York both abused his position as a trade envoy to pass on confidential information to Epstein, as well as further claims that he entertained women who were trafficked to the UK by him. Andrew’s relationship with close Epstein will form part of several investigations.

Article continues below

The former prince Andrew hosted Jeffrey Epstein, his former lover Ghislaine Maxwell and the jailed sex offender Harvey Weinstein at Royal Lodge, the prince's private former home in the grounds of Windsor Castle.

The trio visited the Windsor mansion as part of his daughter Beatrice's masked ball 18th birthday celebrations in 2006, two months after a US arrest warrant had been issued for Epstein for the sexual assault of a minor.

Buckingham Palace was contacted for comment.

News.title: Woman's body discovered in home as police seal off Cork scene
News.content:
Woman's body discovered in home as police seal off Cork scene

The body of a woman was discovered at a property in Ballincollig, Cork this evening. Gardaí have sealed off the scene of what has been described as a 'serious incident'.

View 2 Images Gardaí at the scene of the incident in Ballincollig on Friday evening

Police and emergency services have locked down a residential street after the body of a woman was found.

The woman's body was found at a residence in the Innishmore Park estate in the Cork suburb earlier today.

Local residents have been left reeling following the tragic and heartbreaking discovery. It's believed the woman was aged in her 30s and was a familiar face in the Ballincollig community, reports Cork Beo.

Officers have cordoned off the location of what's been characterised as a profoundly "serious incident."

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

A Garda spokesperson confirmed the investigation remains at an early juncture. They stated: "An Garda Síochána are currently at the scene of a serious incident.

"We are not in a position to provide any further details at this early stage."

View 2 Images Gardaí at scene of incident in Ballincollig (Image: Daire Fitton)

Article continues below

Local Sinn Féin councillor, Joe Lynch, is a resident of Innishmore and has spent the evening with residents near the scene. He shared how the community has been left devastated by the "truly shocking" news.

He said: "In the first instance, my thoughts go out to the victim and their family. This is a truly shocking incident, and it has caused major upset in what is a close, tight-knit community.

"As a lifelong resident of the estate, I have spent the evening with friends and neighbours, and all I can say is that everyone here in Innishmore is shell-shocked. The Gardaí have a job to do now to bring the person responsible to justice, and we know they will do all they can to ensure that is done."

News.title: 'Ramadan is isolating and not for fainthearted but something special helps me'
News.content:
'Ramadan is isolating and not for fainthearted but something special helps me'

As Open Iftars become increasingly popular creating a sense of belonging and unity among different communities, Maryam Qaiser attends one hosted at Notts County football club

View 3 Images Notts County Foundation host Open Iftar (Image: Notts County Foundation)

As Ramadan draws to an end for another year, I always describe the month as a ‘humbling experience’.

‌



Abstaining from food and drink from sunrise to sunset for 29 or 30 days is not for the fainthearted. But when I look back, the month has always been quite an isolating time for me too.

‌



Navigating the usual 9 to 5 work day, managing fatigue and finding pockets of time to quickly pray is tricky. Like many who grew up in non-Muslim country, I often found myself explaining what Ramadan is to people, the bizarre times we get up in the night to eat our meal before the fast starts and that actually we really can’t drink water throughout the day.

‌



But in the last few years, I’ve really started to enjoy Ramadan, which is the holiest month in the Islamic (Hijri) calendar. Supermarkets now have banners wishing shoppers ‘Ramadan Mubarak’, shelves are filled with essentials like dates to break our fast, ingredients to make traditional foods not to mention Eid greeting cards.

View 3 Images Open Iftar at Notts County Stadium where food was served by Saracens restaurant

However what I found most special is the rise of Open Iftars. Across the country, Open Iftars have become a popular event hosted by charities, companies, foodbanks, communities, premier league football clubs and even the Met Police. It is a chance to break fast with people from all walks of life and backgrounds. I’ve met many non-Muslims who’ve kept a fast in solitary with us at these events.

‌



This year I was invited to Notts County FC stadium for their third Open Iftar held. It was hosted by Notts County Foundation and Mercy for All charity. The room was full of people from diverse backgrounds, which sparked conversation and reflection.

The call to pray was given and we all broke fast with a date and water. There was a dedicated room to pray for Muslims before we tucked into a delicious buffet. For me Open Iftars create a real sense of belonging and the power of togetherness through community engagement.

View 3 Images (L-R) Notts County’s striker Alassana Jatta with Naser Hussain, fundraising coordinator at Mercy For All

Article continues below

Vicky Spargo, deputy sport and inclusion manager at Notts County Foundation, says: “Our Open Iftar was about more than just breaking fast - it was about breaking down barriers. Seeing so many members of our community come together at Meadow Lane, sharing stories, faith and food, was powerful. We’re proud to work alongside Mercy for All to create opportunities that champion inclusion, celebrate diversity and ensure everyone feels they belong within our Foundation.”

Funds were also raised for Mercy for All, which provides education and support to orphans, women and families in need both locally and globally. Notts County’s striker Alassana Jatta also attended the event.

Zarmeena Quarishi, ambassador for the charity, says: “This evening has been great, it allows people to embrace and learn about our culture and faith as well as bringing people together. With everything that is going on right now, we need more initiatives like this that bring people together in Britain, community unity is really needed right now.”

News.title: Jermaine Jenas puts family home up for sale after BBC axe from £190k-a-year role
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Jermaine Jenas puts family home up for sale after BBC axe from £190k-a-year role

The former footballer left his role at the broadcaster, understood to be worth £190,000-a-year, after he was caught up in a high-profile cheating scandal in August 2024

View 4 Images Jermaine Jenas has put his family home up for sale (Image: Getty Images)

Jermaine Jenas has listed his family home for sale - 18 months after being axed from his £190,000-a-year BBC role for sending inappropriate texts to female staff members.

‌



The former One Show co-host and ex-Match of the Day pundit has put the six-bedroom property, near Barnet, North London, on the market for £4.95million. Pictures of the property posted on an estate agency website show the des-res includes an indoor swimming pool, a sauna and a steam room, as well as a gym, a cinema room and a playroom.

‌



Jenas, 43, his wife Ellie, 37, and their children had lived there together after he bought the property for £2.8million in 2006, but they split up after his cheating scandal was revealed in August 2024. He has since listed his newly set-up company Coldline Ltd at a £3million terraced house in Hampstead, but is not believed to be living at the address.

‌



View 4 Images Jermaine and Ellie, whose maiden name was Penfold, got married in 2011 (Image: Instagram)

At the time of the scandal, Jenas admitted sending suggestive messages to two female colleagues on The One Show and apologised to the women who received them.

Speaking at the time, the dad-of-four admitted Ellie, who he shares three young children with, was "absolutely raging" after finding out about the messages and had immediately kicked him out of the bedroom of the family home. He also confessed to lying to her during a family holiday in Marbella, Spain, when he snuck off to have a Zoom call with BBC bosses about the inappropriate messages.

‌



He said he later came clean to her that he had been fired because she was expecting him to go to work on The One Show on the Monday morning. The married former Tottenham and England footballer insisted he did nothing illegal and the messages were between consenting adults, including one he claimed made clear she was interested.

Recalling the moment he confessed to his wife, he said: "I just said, 'I've been sacked from the BBC'. She said, 'What for?' and I said, 'For sending text messages that weren't appropriate to two girls'.

View 4 Images Ellie enjoyed a holiday to Dubai, shortly before the start of the Middle East war (Image: Ellie Jenas/Instagram)

‌



At the time, Jenas’ dad Dennis told the Mirror his son was desperate to save his marriage to Ellie, admitting his sacking had left him in a mess emotionally. Speaking from his home in Fort Worth, Texas, he said: "He's battling to save his marriage. It's hit the family hard at the end of the day. He knows he shouldn't have done what he did and he wants to keep his family together and he knows he has hurt his wife and kids."

Jenas was hit with more claims the following month, including accusations of "repeatedly pestering" a junior make-up artist for sex while working at BT Sport and allegedly bombarding a woman working at the World Cup in Qatar with messages, including one asking her: "You want my boy now?".

In March last year, Ellie announced their marriage was over. Writing on Instagram, she said: "I never imagined I would have to share something so personal with the public, but given the situation, I feel it's necessary. After 16 years together and 4 wonderful children, Jermaine and I have decided to part ways. We will remain friends and continue to co-parent. We kindly ask that you respect our children's privacy during this challenging time. Thank you, Ellie."

‌



Jenas had long been considered a rising star at the BBC and was touted by Gary Lineker as a future Match of the Day host. Lineker had heralded Jenas as his "natural successor". However, his fall from grace left him out of the running and he later admitted his TV career had been "wiped out".

View 4 Images At the time of the cheating scandal in August 2024, Jermaine admitted Ellie was "absolutely raging" and had kicked him out of the bedroom of the family home (Image: Daily Mirror)

The retired footballer and Ellie got married in 2011 and have two daughters and a young son together. Jenas also has an older teenage daughter Sancha from a previous relationship. She reportedly lives in the United States with her mother.

‌



Speaking in November 2024 while launching her own business Preppy Skin, Ellie admitted the scandal had been "incredibly difficult". She added her "only focus has been on our children" and she was keen "to move forward" as she began her new venture.

She said: "Naturally this has been hard for me but my only concern has been our children and that will not change. Now I just want to move forward." She added Jenas had been "supportive" of her decision to create the brand and is "excited" for what it holds for her in the future.

In December last year, Jenas spoke about how the split had hit him hard. Appearing on YouTube channel Golf Life with fellow former footballer Jimmy Bullard and ex-Soccer AM star Peter Dale, known as Tubes, Jenas said: "I think it's common knowledge, I had a tough probably year... I obviously split up with my wife and stuff.

Article continues below

"As a man you go through things sometimes in life, you need support from friends and so on and so forth... something just hit me." Jenas said he felt unable to play golf at the time, as he considers it "a luxury".

He added: "At that particular time my whole world was blowing up. It just didn't feel right if you know what I mean. It felt like I needed to be you know with my kids and with the family and trying to just sort things out basically.

"I started doing all these things I'd never done before just to try and steady the ship. But um yeah, the clubs are back out. I'm feeling good. I'll be fine."

News.title: Young driver jailed after partying and TikToks following fatal Lincolnshire crash
News.content:
Young driver jailed after partying and TikToks following fatal Lincolnshire crash

Madeleine Lonsdale, 18, sped along a carriageway while racing another car at 100mph - before slamming into a tree and killing passengers Harrison Carter, 18, and George Stephenson, 17

View 4 Images Madeleine Lonsdale, 18, has been sentenced to 14 months' detention (Image: PA)

A woman went on a skiing holiday and partied "repeatedly" after her car slammed into a tree and killed two teenagers.

‌



Madeleine Lonsdale had been speeding along a carriageway at 100mph in Marston, Lincolnshire, while racing another car in June last year. The 18-year-old then struck a tree, killing passengers Harrison Carter, 18, and George Stephenson, 17.

‌



Lonsdale frequently uploaded to TikTok after the deaths, and her first court appearance was delayed for two weeks because she went on a skiing trip. She was today sentenced at Lincoln Crown Court to 14 months imprisonment and banned from driving for three and a half years.

‌



View 4 Images Harrison Carter was killed in the crash on Toll Bar Road, Marston, Lincolnshire (Image: PA)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

On Friday, June 20, 2025, Lonsdale was travelling towards Marston from the A1 at around 1.40am when the Kia Niro 4 EV she was driving left the carriageway, colliding with a tree on a right-hand bend. Harrison, 18, who was in the passenger seat, and George, 17, who was sat in the back, sadly died at the scene from their injuries.

‌



Lonsdale had been out with friends when she stopped at the Esso service station on the junction of the A1 and Toll Bar Road. Having purchased some items, they got back in the car and then turned left onto Toll Bar Road towards Marston, before a short time later she lost control and collided with a tree.

Detectives found that Lonsdale had been driving at over 75mph when she misjudged a bend in the 60mph road just 4 seconds before the collision occurred. Her vehicle had left the carriageway to the nearside verge and subsequently impacted the tree.

Lonsdale, who passed her driving test around 12 weeks before the crash, escaped with only a small injury to her wrist.

‌



View 4 Images George Stephenson, 17, was also another passenger killed in the incident (Image: PA)

In a police interview, the teenager said she knew the road and believed she had been doing around 45mph. She was also unable to explain why her wheels had come off the road. The investigation concluded that Lonsdale's driving, speed and inexperience were responsible for the collision.

Harry's mum, Sarah Carter, said it "adds insult to injury" that Lonsdale was "partying repeatedly" and "choreographing TikToks" while she planned her son's funeral.

‌



Speaking of Harry's dream to pursue a career in law, she said: "Harry was taken from us because Madeleine chose to speed. He will never be able to celebrate the amazing exam results that he achieved."

Victoria Stephenson, George's mother, told the court: "We will never see him graduate or find a job after university. We didn't get to celebrate his 18th birthday with him. Instead, we had his funeral."

She said that when Lonsdale's skiing trip delaying her first court appearance by two weeks, it "only added to the torment".

‌



View 4 Images The incident on Toll Bar Road saw the car, driven at speed by Lonsdale, crash into a tree (Image: PA)

In a joint statement, the families of George and Harrison said: "We recognise the sentence of the court, but the reality is that no sentence will ever bring our beloved sons back to us. Madeleine alone knows why she chose to drive at over 75mph in the dark on an unlit country lane. She will have to live with the fact that she is responsible for taking the lives of our boys, who had placed their trust in her to get them to their destination safely.

"As parents, we therefore implore all young drivers to take care when driving and to remember their personal responsibility for ensuring the safety of themselves, their passengers and others around them. Whilst we now wish for privacy after a long and difficult journey, we just hope that others never have to face the despair that is ahead of us."

Article continues below

John McNally, defending Lonsdale, said: "She knows that there is nothing she can say, do, to take the pain away. She knows it will weigh heavily on her for the rest of her life. Her regret for what happened is deep and [...] her friends will remain, and are, in her thoughts."

Detective Sergeant Adam Doona, from our Serious Collision Investigation Unit, said: "This collision was down to the speed and inexperience of Madeleine Lonsdale. Tragically, as a result of her driving, friends who should have been celebrating starting the next stage of their lives are no longer with us.

"When someone obtains their full driving licence, they need to remember that this is purely the start of their driving career. A lack of experience and failing to drive according to the rules of the road in this case, will leave an indelible mark on all those involved for the rest of their lives."

News.title: Prince William shows true colours in promise to Kate amid Andrew and Epstein scandal
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Prince William shows true colours in promise to Kate amid Andrew and Epstein scandal

With Mother's Day around the corner, the Princess of Wales will want to honour her own mum, Carole, to whom Prince William once made an important promise to

View 4 Images The Prince and Princess of Wales (Image: PA)

The Prince and Princess of Wales have certainly had their trials and tribulations over the past two years. First they had to deal with Kate's unexpected cancer diagnosis and subsequent chemotherapy treatment. While now, alongside the King, they are having to deal with the fallout for the monarchy surrounding the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and the Epstein scandal.

But one person both William and Kate know they can rely on for both practical and emotional support is Kate's mum, Carole. The grandmother-of-seven is often described as the future Queen's 'rock', playing a pivotal role behind the scenes in what has been a turbulent period for the Royal Family.

Kate Middleton gives health update and reveals what she's cut back since cancer diagnosis READ MORE :

Huge Queen anniversary marked with unique collection by iconic British brand READ MORE :

View 4 Images William and Kate the last time they were seen in public with Andrew (Image: WireImage)

And while this worry facing the royals would naturally make Carole be concerned about how the situation could be affecting her daughter, royal expert Jennie Bond believes the promise William once made to his mum-in-law will put her mind at rest.

The former BBC royal correspondent told the Mirror : "It’s been reported, and I believe it’s true, that William once promised Mike and Carole that he would protect Catherine and always look after her. And he repeated that vow to a member of the public at one of his first engagements after Catherine’s cancer diagnosis. I’m sure that has earned him the respect of his in-laws."

On Sunday, the UK celebrates Mother's Day, with Kate no doubt wanting to honour mum Carole in a special way. And Jennie believes that William, too will recognise the family role she plays. She added: "What seems fairly unusual in the relationship is that William is perhaps his mother-in-law’s biggest fan.

View 4 Images The Princess of Wales with her parents in December 2006 (Image: Tim Graham Photo Library via Getty Images)

"He warmed to Carole from the start, and loved the easy atmosphere in the family home and the relaxed Sunday lunches they would enjoy together — and no doubt still do. William was probably only just coming to terms with the traumatic loss of his mother when Catherine walked into his life, and , with her, came Carole and a normal, relaxed family who welcomed him into their home.

"For William, it was a breath of fresh air - a touch of uncomplicated normality in his complex life. And I’m sure Catherine loved her parents all the more for making William feel so at home. He respects Carole and loves her high energy and tenacity."

Unlike their daughter, Carole and Michael generally keep a low profile, living life away from the spotlight. However, it has been plain to see just how much of a presence the couple has been when it comes to supporting Kate, William and their children. The couple have been to every one of their daughter's Christmas Carol Concerts at Westminster Abbey to quietly, yet proudly support her.

View 4 Images Carole with Princess Anne and Queen Camilla at the Cheltenham Festival (Image: Shutterstock)

Then, when Kate released a heartwarming video in September 2024 to announce the end of her chemotherapy treatment, it featured her parents, who were seen in the Wales family kitchen in Amner Hall playing a game of cards with George, Charlotte and Louis. However, earlier this week, Carole did make a high profile outing to the Cheltenham Festival, where she enjoyed a day at the races.

She seemed to be fully embraced into the royal inner circle, watching the action alongside Queen Camilla, linking arms with Mike and Zara Tindall and sharing a joke with Princess Anne.

And Jennie explained: "At Cheltenham, it was obvious that her relationship with Catherine’s royal relatives is relaxed and comfortable. Camilla looked pleased to see her, Anne was all smiles, Zara was clearly delighted to share a giggle with her, and Carole seemed totally in her element. She may not have a title or an official royal role, but she is very much one of the family now.

"I’m sure they all recognise how vital Carole has been in supporting Catherine — through the good times and the worrying ones. And you have to remember that she herself was carrying a huge burden of worry over her beloved daughter battling cancer.

"They all know that she is important, too, in William’s life. Carole has been the lynchpin in the Wales’s family as they navigated the repercussions of Catherine’s cancer diagnosis, offering both practical and emotional support.

"Carole and her husband, Mike, have provided an invaluable role model of a happy, stable family for William and the children. It’s something that was sadly lacking for William when he was young, but thanks to the Middletons, he’s found it in later life — and that augurs well for all of them."

Article continues below

News.content: Lord Mandelson has repeatedly let it be known that he believes he has not acted criminally. He has long argued that he accepted Epstein and his lawyer's version of events and only discovered the truth after his death in 2019.

News.content: The man paid to send them, Alexander Suranovas, has told the BBC he had no idea there were explosives inside. He also revealed that when he was arrested, he'd already been hired to send "three or four" more parcels every month.

News.title: Towie star's plea for mandatory ice skating gloves rejected
News.content: In an emotional Instagram post, Lewis explained how she received a call alerting her to Beau having been injured at an ice rink when he fell over and someone "ran over his finger with their bladed skates, which took his finger off".

News.title: F1 races in Middle East to be cancelled because of war in Iran
News.content:
The Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Grands Prix that were scheduled for next month are set to be cancelled as a result of the war in the Middle East.

A formal decision to call off the races has not yet been made but is expected before the end of the weekend.

Freight would need to start being shipped to the Middle East in the coming days. With no sign of the conflict between the US/Israel and Iran coming to a conclusion, holding the races would put personnel at too great a risk.

Neither event will be replaced, with the season being cut to 22 grands prix and F1 taking a commercial hit of more than Â£100m, given Bahrain and Saudi Arabia pay two of the highest hosting fees.

The race in Bahrain was scheduled to be on 12 April with Jeddah the following weekend.

Consideration was given to holding events at Portimao in Portugal, Imola in Italy or Istanbul Park in Turkey.

But it was accepted that the time to organise a race at any of those locations was too short, and there was little chance of securing a hosting fee.

The decision will mean there is a five-week break between the Japanese Grand Prix on 29 March and Miami on 3 May.

Boxing.title: Jake Paul suffers embarrassing hygiene gaffe during Donald Trump rally appearance as major wardrobe error exposes long-held insecurity
Boxing.content:
YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump at a political rally.

Paul joined Trump on stage at Verst Logistics in Kentucky and was given the chance to address the crowd.

The 29-year-old went viral on social media, not for his comments about Trump and American manufacturing, but instead about his appearance.

Many pointed out vast sweat patches that were visible on Paul's beige suit as he spoke to the crowd.

The patches were particularly prominent under his armpits, which were exposed further when he raised his arms to the crowd.

Paul had previously spoken about how sweating had long been a major insecurity of his.

Jake Paul gets on stage at the Trump rally and has the nastiest pit stains I've ever seen



(it also appears the crowd doesn't know who he is) pic.twitter.com/3Yc071tZzf — Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) March 11, 2026

YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump

Paul and his brother Logan had promoted his deodorant brand ahead of his fight with Mike Tyson back in 2024

Paul made the revelation back in 2024 when he launched a deodorant brand W, which he and his brother Logan promoted before and after his bout with Mike Tyson.

'Sweating has been a longtime insecurity of mine,' Paul had said when launching the brand.

'If you know me, you know I'm a clean freak. I always want to smell fresh, but training in Puerto Rico's humid climate makes it a real challenge.

'I needed a product that could keep up with my lifestyle — one that smells amazing but isn't full of junk ingredients, and honestly, one that wasn't from 2006.

'That's why I started W… to create products for guys that will boost their confidence and smell amazing, but also don't cost $20 for a stick of deodorant.'

The W deodorants are claimed to offer 24 hours protection, with the brand website stating: 'With doubled scent load, increased hydration and more sweat-blocking power, our new deodorant and body wash are a monumental breakthrough for mankind (or at least for your morning)'.

During the event, Trump signalled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

Join the debate Did YOU spot Jake Paul's hygiene gaffe?

Trump had brought Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday in Kentucky

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

'And you have my complete and total endorsement.'

Paul had expressed his admiration for Trump credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul added. And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us.

'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'



The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavours in the boxing ring over recent years.

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision for the American Heartland.

'And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us,' he continued, signaling his belief in a resurgence of American manufacturing.

The fighter also leaned into a spiritual message, telling the audience: 'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.'

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless,' Paul concluded, before reflecting on the local spirit of the attendees in the Bluegrass State.

'I feel all the local Kentuckians feel the same way -- you guys have that fight, you guys have that swag. There's a lot of young kids in here. The future of America.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision

Paul's most recent endeavor in the boxing ring saw him take on British star Anthony Joshua

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavors in the boxing ring over recent years.

Paul has transformed the boxing landscape by leveraging his massive digital following to create events that have outdrawn traditional championship bouts in viewership and revenue.

His most recent endeavor in the ring saw him take on British heavyweight star Anthony Joshua, which ended in a knockout victory for 'AJ'.

Whether Paul follows Trump's advice and swaps the gloves for a suit remains to be seen.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone

Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.

The former heavyweight world champion, 36, has formally confirmed he will now be resident in the United Arab Emirates despite it currently coming under nightly attack from Iranian suicide drones.

Joshua, who was born in Watford of Nigerian heritage, won a gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics and has since been awarded both OBE and MBE honours - but now is quitting his home country.

The surprise move by 'AJ' - as he is known to fans - is revealed in the small print of paperwork he filed on Friday for his £150million business empire in which he is legally required to state the country in which he lives.

And, extraordinarily, the documentation shows that Joshua has pressed ahead with his decision to change his residential status despite the war between the USA and Israel and Iran which has seen neighbouring UAE come under sustained attack.

Anthony Joshua is moving to Dubai - as revealed in the small print of his paperwork

Footage taken from social media shows Saturday's drone strike on Dubai International Airport

As well as the weather and facilities, Dubai is popular with the wealthy because there is no income tax there.

Joshua would have been liable to pay UK tax on his dividend payment of £10.1million in 2024 and £6.3million in 2023.

His company Sparta Promotions also made profits of £20.396million and paid £6.65million in UK tax in 2024.

Joshua confirmed the decision in the same week that recent UK sport emigre Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate spoke about what it is like to live in Dubai while it is under missile attack.

Those attacks have seen thousands of Britons living in or visiting Dubai when the war broke out left desperate to get out - with some paying as much as £100,000 to secure evacuation on a private jet.

Filings for two of Joshua's companies, Sparta Promotions Limited and 258 Investments Limited, both show the formal change in where he will from now on reside.
